evals.name: core-nextjs-voice
evals.prompt: I have a Next.js 16 App Router app (TypeScript, React 19). Add a voice assistant using xAI's Grok realtime voice API so users can talk to it from the browser — click a button, speak, hear it answer. Assume auth is already handled (you can stub a getAuthenticatedUser() helper). Create all the files needed.
evals.expected_output: Token-mint route (POST api.x.ai/v1/realtime/client_secrets, server-side), client that opens wss://api.x.ai/v1/realtime with the token as subprotocol xai-client-secret.<token>, sends session.update first, streams PCM16@24kHz base64 mic audio, plays response audio gaplessly with barge-in.

evals.name: function-calling
evals.prompt: Build me a voice agent with xAI (Grok voice, the realtime WebSocket API) for my Next.js app that can call a function to book appointments when the caller asks for one. The booking should happen on my server, not in the browser. Stub the actual database write. Create all files needed.
evals.expected_output: Everything from the core path plus: tool definitions in session.update, buffering of response.function_call_arguments.done events, execution on response.done via an authed server route, a function_call_output for every call (failures become {error} outputs), then exactly one response.create.

evals.name: key-safety-token-arch
evals.prompt: How do I keep my xAI API key safe if the browser has to open the websocket to x.ai for their realtime voice API? I'm on Vercel with a Next.js app. Explain the approach and set it up for me.
evals.expected_output: Explains ephemeral tokens: server route mints via POST /v1/realtime/client_secrets with Authorization: Bearer XAI_API_KEY and short expiry, browser receives only the ephemeral token and passes it as WebSocket subprotocol; XAI_API_KEY stays server-only. Implements the token route.
